internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Spec.TestIndex

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Spec.Witness

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Spec.Realizes

internalDeclarations.type: ∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT1.Spec P) (G : P.Ambient), CT1.Target S G ↔ ∃ index witness, S.Realizes G index witness

internalDeclarations.type: {P : Core.Problem} → CT1.Spec P → CT1.Input P → Prop
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.EquivalenceState

internalDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {S : CT1.Spec P} → CT1.Capability S → (G : P.Ambient) → (index : S.TestIndex) → FinEnum (S.Witness G index)
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.witnesses

internalDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {S : CT1.Spec P} →
    CT1.Capability S →
      (G : P.Ambient) → (index : S.TestIndex) → (witness : S.Witness G index) → Decidable (S.Realizes G index witness)
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.realizesDecidable

internalDeclarations.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {S : CT1.Spec P} → CT1.Capability S → P.Ambient → ℕ
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.inputSize
internalDeclarations.docString: Application-specific input size used to audit the local CT1 schedule.

internalDeclarations.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {S : CT1.Spec P} → CT1.Capability S → ℕ
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.workCoefficient
internalDeclarations.docString: Coefficient in the certified polynomial work bound.

internalDeclarations.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {S : CT1.Spec P} → CT1.Capability S → ℕ
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.workDegree
internalDeclarations.docString: Degree in the certified polynomial work bound.

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {S : CT1.Spec P} (self : CT1.Capability S) (G : P.Ambient),
  CT1.searchCheckBound S self.tests self.witnesses G ≤ self.workCoefficient * (self.inputSize G + 1) ^ self.workDegree
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.workBound
internalDeclarations.docString: The complete local schedule is polynomial in the declared input size.

internalDeclarations.type:
{ι : Type u} →
  {α : ι → Type v} →
    Core.DependentEnumeration ι α →
      (p : (i : ι) → α i → Prop) →
        ((index : ι) → (value : α index) → Decidable (p index value)) → Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult p
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.dependentSearch
internalDeclarations.docString:
Exhaustive nested search over a finite index enumeration and an exact
finite enumeration for each dependent fibre.

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {S : CT1.Spec P} {input : CT1.Input P} (equivalence : CT1.EquivalenceState S input),
  (∀ (index : S.TestIndex) (witness : S.Witness input.context.G index), ¬S.Realizes input.context.G index witness) →
    CT1.AvoidingState S input equivalence
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.AvoidingState.ofNoRealization

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {ι : Type u} {α : ι → Type v} (enumeration : Core.DependentEnumeration ι α) (p : (i : ι) → α i → Prop)
  (decideP : (index : ι) → (value : α index) → Decidable (p index value)),
  match Core.FiniteSearch.dependentSearch enumeration p decideP with
  | Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.found index value holds => p index value
  | Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.absent none => True
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.dependentSearch_sound

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {ι : Type u} {α : ι → Type v} (enumeration : Core.DependentEnumeration ι α) (p : (i : ι) → α i → Prop)
  (decideP : (index : ι) → (value : α index) → Decidable (p index value)),
  (∃ index value, p index value) →
    match Core.FiniteSearch.dependentSearch enumeration p decideP with
    | Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.found index value holds => True
    | Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.absent none => False
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.dependentSearch_complete

internalDeclarations.type: {P : Core.Problem} → (S : CT1.Spec P) → (input : CT1.Input P) → CT1.EquivalenceState S input → Type (max u_3 u_4)

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.C1Certificate
internalDeclarations.docString: Provenance-indexed C1 evidence.

internalDeclarations.type: Type (max (uAmbient + 1) (uBranch + 1))
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem
internalDeclarations.docString:
Mathematical data shared by every tactic in one structural-exhaustion
proof instance.

internalDeclarations.type: Core.Problem → Type (max (max (uI + 1) (uW + 1)) u_1)
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Spec
internalDeclarations.docString:
Mathematical target-test vocabulary.  It contains no search procedure,
downstream tactic, or completed node decision.

internalDeclarations.type: Core.Problem → Type uAmbient
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.Problem.Ambient

internalDeclarations.type: {P : Core.Problem} → CT1.Spec P → P.Ambient → Prop
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Target
internalDeclarations.docString:
The canonical CT1 target.  Defining the target from its realization
semantics makes target-test equivalence a framework theorem by reflexivity.

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {S : CT1.Spec P} {input : CT1.Input P},
  (CT1.Target S input.context.G ↔ ∃ index witness, S.Realizes input.context.G index witness) →
    CT1.EquivalenceState S input
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.EquivalenceState.mk

internalDeclarations.type: {P : Core.Problem} → CT1.Spec P → Type (max (max u_1 u_3) u_4)
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability
internalDeclarations.docString:
The complete problem-specific surface needed to execute CT1.

The capability contains only finite enumerators and a decision procedure for
the primitive realization relation. Search, exhaustiveness, certificates,
paths, and terminal selection are framework-derived.

internalDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  (S : CT1.Spec P) →
    FinEnum S.TestIndex → ((G : P.Ambient) → (index : S.TestIndex) → FinEnum (S.Witness G index)) → P.Ambient → ℕ
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.searchCheckBound
internalDeclarations.docString:
Number of primitive realization decisions made by the exhaustive CT1
schedule when every finite fibre is inspected.  This counts only the local
tests supplied by the capability; it does not construct any ambient universe.

internalDeclarations.type: (ι : Type u) → (ι → Type v) → Type (max u v)
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.DependentEnumeration
internalDeclarations.docString:
An exact enumeration of a dependent family over an exact finite index
universe.

internalDeclarations.type: {ι : Type u} → {α : ι → Type v} → Core.DependentEnumeration ι α → (i : ι) → FinEnum (α i)
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.DependentEnumeration.fibres

internalDeclarations.type: {ι : Type u} → {α : ι → Type v} → Core.DependentEnumeration ι α → FinEnum ι
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.DependentEnumeration.indices

internalDeclarations.type: {ι : Type u} → {α : ι → Type v} → ((i : ι) → α i → Prop) → Type (max u v)
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult
internalDeclarations.docString:
Dependent exhaustive-search result.  The witness is constructed directly
in `Type`; it is never extracted from a propositional existential.

internalDeclarations.type:
{ι : Type u} →
  {α : ι → Type v} →
    {p : (i : ι) → α i → Prop} → (∀ (index : ι) (value : α index), ¬p index value) → Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult p
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.absent

internalDeclarations.type:
{ι : Type u} →
  {α : ι → Type v} →
    {p : (i : ι) → α i → Prop} → (index : ι) → (value : α index) → p index value → Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult p
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.found

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {ι : Type u_1} {α : ι → Type u_2} (enumeration : Core.DependentEnumeration ι α) (p : (i : ι) → α i → Prop),
  (∀ index ∈ enumeration.indices.orderedValues, ∀ (value : α index), ¬p index value) →
    ∀ (index : ι) (value : α index), ¬p index value
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.dependentSearch._proof_1

internalDeclarations.type:
{ι : Type u_1} →
  {α : ι → Type u_2} →
    (enumeration : Core.DependentEnumeration ι α) →
      (p : (i : ι) → α i → Prop) →
        (motive :
            StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentListResult✝ enumeration.indices.orderedValues p →
              Sort u_3) →
          (x : StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentListResult✝ enumeration.indices.orderedValues p) →
            ((index : ι) →
                (member : index ∈ enumeration.indices.orderedValues) →
                  (value : α index) →
                    (holds : p index value) →
                      motive
                        (StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentListResult.found✝ index member value holds)) →
              ((absentProof : ∀ index ∈ enumeration.indices.orderedValues, ∀ (value : α index), ¬p index value) →
                  motive (StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentListResult.absent✝ absentProof)) →
                motive x
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.dependentSearch.match_1

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {S : CT1.Spec P} {input : CT1.Input P} {equivalence : CT1.EquivalenceState S input},
  (∀ (index : S.TestIndex) (witness : S.Witness input.context.G index), ¬S.Realizes input.context.G index witness) →
    CT1.AvoidingState S input equivalence
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.AvoidingState.mk

internalDeclarations.type: {P : Core.Problem} → CT1.Input P → Core.BranchContext P
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Input.context

internalDeclarations.type: {P : Core.Problem} → Core.BranchContext P → P.Ambient
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.BranchContext.G

internalDeclarations.type:
{ι : Type u} →
  {α : ι → Type v} →
    {p : (i : ι) → α i → Prop} →
      {motive : Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult p → Sort u_1} →
        (t : Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult p) →
          ((index : ι) →
              (value : α index) →
                (holds : p index value) → motive (Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.found index value holds)) →
            ((none : ∀ (index : ι) (value : α index), ¬p index value) →
                motive (Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.absent none)) →
              motive t
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.casesOn

internalDeclarations.type:
{ι : Type u_1} →
  {α : ι → Type u_2} →
    {p : (i : ι) → α i → Prop} →
      (motive : Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult p → Sort u_3) →
        (x : Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult p) →
          ((index : ι) →
              (value : α index) →
                (holds : p index value) → motive (Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.found index value holds)) →
            ((none : ∀ (index : ι) (value : α index), ¬p index value) →
                motive (Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.absent none)) →
              motive x
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.index?.match_1

internalDeclarations.type:

internalDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  (S : CT1.Spec P) →
    (input : CT1.Input P) →
      (motive : Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult (S.Realizes input.context.G) → Sort u_5) →
        (x : Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult (S.Realizes input.context.G)) →
          ((index : S.TestIndex) →
              (witness : S.Witness input.context.G index) →
                (realizes : S.Realizes input.context.G index witness) →
                  motive (Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.found index witness realizes)) →
            ((noRealization :
                  ∀ (index : S.TestIndex) (value : S.Witness input.context.G index),
                    ¬S.Realizes input.context.G index value) →
                motive (Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.absent noRealization)) →
              motive x

                motive t

presentation.plainExplanation: The finite family of target tests that CT1 must consider.

presentation.label: Realization witness

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Spec.Witness

presentation.plainExplanation: The proposition saying that witness w realizes test i in G.
presentation.label: Realization predicate

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → (self : CT1.Spec P) → (G : P.Ambient) → (index : self.TestIndex) → self.Witness G index → Prop
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Spec.Realizes

presentation.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of all test indices.
presentation.label: Finite test enumeration

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {S : CT1.Spec P} → CT1.Capability S → FinEnum S.TestIndex
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.tests

presentation.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of the witnesses for each object and test.
presentation.label: Finite witness enumeration

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {S : CT1.Spec P} → CT1.Capability S → (G : P.Ambient) → (index : S.TestIndex) → FinEnum (S.Witness G index)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.witnesses

presentation.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding whether a proposed witness realizes a test.
presentation.label: Realization decision procedure

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {S : CT1.Spec P} →
    CT1.Capability S →
      (G : P.Ambient) → (index : S.TestIndex) → (witness : S.Witness G index) → Decidable (S.Realizes G index witness)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.realizesDecidable

presentation.plainExplanation: The application-specific size measure used in CT1's complexity certificate.
presentation.label: Input size

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {S : CT1.Spec P} → CT1.Capability S → P.Ambient → ℕ
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.inputSize

presentation.plainExplanation: The coefficient in the certified polynomial bound on exhaustive checks.
presentation.label: Work coefficient

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {S : CT1.Spec P} → CT1.Capability S → ℕ
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.workCoefficient

presentation.plainExplanation: The degree in the certified polynomial bound on exhaustive checks.
presentation.label: Work degree

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {S : CT1.Spec P} → CT1.Capability S → ℕ
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.workDegree

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that the complete CT1 search schedule satisfies the declared polynomial bound.
presentation.label: Polynomial work bound

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {S : CT1.Spec P} (self : CT1.Capability S) (G : P.Ambient),
  CT1.searchCheckBound S self.tests self.witnesses G ≤ self.workCoefficient * (self.inputSize G + 1) ^ self.workDegree
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.Capability.workBound

presentation.plainExplanation: The profile's finite code type for witnesses of the public target on G.
presentation.label: Target code

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} → CT1.TargetEncoding PublicTarget → P.Ambient → Type uCode
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetEncoding.Code

presentation.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of the target codes for G.
presentation.label: Finite code enumeration

{P : Core.Problem} →
  {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} → (self : CT1.TargetEncoding PublicTarget) → (G : P.Ambient) → FinEnum (self.Code G)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetEncoding.codes

presentation.plainExplanation: The proposition saying that code c is a valid target certificate for G.
presentation.label: Code acceptance

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} → (self : CT1.TargetEncoding PublicTarget) → (G : P.Ambient) → self.Code G → Prop
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetEncoding.Accepts

presentation.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding whether a target code is accepted.
presentation.label: Acceptance decision procedure

formalDeclaration.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (self : CT1.TargetEncoding PublicTarget) → (G : P.Ambient) → (code : self.Code G) → Decidable (self.Accepts G code)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetEncoding.acceptsDecidable

presentation.plainExplanation: The public-target profile's size measure for G.
presentation.label: Encoded input size

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} → CT1.TargetEncoding PublicTarget → P.Ambient → ℕ
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetEncoding.inputSize

presentation.plainExplanation: The coefficient bounding the target-code enumeration.
presentation.label: Encoded work coefficient

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} → CT1.TargetEncoding PublicTarget → ℕ
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetEncoding.workCoefficient

presentation.plainExplanation: The polynomial degree bounding the target-code enumeration.
presentation.label: Encoded work degree

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} → CT1.TargetEncoding PublicTarget → ℕ
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetEncoding.workDegree

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that enumerating all target codes meets the profile's polynomial bound.
presentation.label: Encoded work bound

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} (self : CT1.TargetEncoding PublicTarget) (G : P.Ambient),
  (self.codes G).orderedValues.length ≤ self.workCoefficient * (self.inputSize G + 1) ^ self.workDegree
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetEncoding.workBound

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that every public-target witness yields an accepted finite code.
presentation.label: Target encoder

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} (self : CT1.TargetEncoding PublicTarget) {G : P.Ambient},
  PublicTarget G → ∃ code, self.Accepts G code

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that every accepted code establishes the public mathematical target.
presentation.label: Target decoder

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} (self : CT1.TargetEncoding PublicTarget) {G : P.Ambient}
  {code : self.Code G}, self.Accepts G code → PublicTarget G
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetEncoding.decode

presentation.plainExplanation: The proof-carrying target code supplied directly to a local CT1 execution.

  {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} →
    (self : CT1.TargetCertificateEncoding PublicTarget) → (G : P.Ambient) → self.Code G → Prop
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetCertificateEncoding.Accepts

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that every public target has a corresponding local certificate code.

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {PublicTarget : P.Ambient → Prop} (self : CT1.TargetCertificateEncoding PublicTarget)

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT1.TargetCertificateEncoding.encode

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that each accepted local certificate establishes the public target.

steps.plainExplanation: Assigns to each local piece its exact boundary interface.
steps.label: Piece interface

steps.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of the outside contexts for each interface.
steps.label: Finite context enumeration

steps.plainExplanation: Reconstructs an ambient object by placing an abstract piece into an outside context.
steps.label: Gluing operation

steps.plainExplanation: Extracts the abstract interior represented by a concrete piece.
steps.label: Piece abstraction

steps.plainExplanation: Extracts the outside environment surrounding the concrete piece in G.
steps.label: Current outside context

steps.plainExplanation: A proof that the context and abstraction recover the original ambient object exactly.
steps.label: Reconstruction law

steps.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding the problem's baseline property on an ambient object.
steps.label: Baseline decision procedure

steps.plainExplanation: Executable evidence deciding the target property on an ambient object.
steps.label: Target decision procedure

steps.plainExplanation: The type of candidate replacements available for a concrete piece p.
steps.label: Replacement candidate

steps.plainExplanation: An exhaustive ordered enumeration of candidate replacements for p.
steps.label: Finite replacement enumeration

steps.plainExplanation: Interprets candidate r as an abstract piece with the same interface as p.
steps.label: Replacement interpretation

steps.plainExplanation: A proof that every enumerated replacement produces a strictly smaller ambient object.
steps.label: Replacement decrease proof

steps.plainExplanation: Executable instance inferred by Lean for this node: DecidableEq StructuralExhaustion.Core.ExactObservation.
steps.label: DecidableEq StructuralExhaustion.Core.ExactObservation

steps.plainExplanation: Failure of deletion to produce a smaller counterexample.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.DeletionCriticalState

steps.plainExplanation: Exhaustively compare a candidate with the source in every exact context.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.analyzeCandidate

steps.plainExplanation:
Turn exhaustive absence of an equivalent candidate into a complete table
of concrete separating contexts.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.replacementTable

steps.plainExplanation: Framework-owned operation executed inside this node: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementSystem.asSmaller.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementSystem.asSmaller

steps.plainExplanation: Exhaustive search over an explicit Mathlib finite enumeration.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.search

steps.plainExplanation: Framework theorem certifying the semantic correctness of this node: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.replacementTable_complete.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.replacementTable_complete

steps.plainExplanation: State, decision, certificate, or residual produced by this node: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementDecision.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementDecision

steps.plainExplanation: A legal replacement whose exact response agrees in every context.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementWitness

steps.plainExplanation:
Complete exhaustive replacement table: every legal candidate has a named
first separating context.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.ReplacementTable

steps.plainExplanation:
A deterministic separating residual.  The complete table is retained so
the residual certifies that no later candidate was silently ignored.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.SeparatingContextResidual

steps.plainExplanation:
Exhaustive criticality is emitted exactly when the legal replacement
universe is empty.
steps.label: StructuralExhaustion.CT2.CriticalityResidual

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} (capability : CT2.Capability P Target)
  (ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target) (input : CT2.Input capability ctx),
  match CT2.analyzeDeletion capability ctx input with
  | CT2.DeletionDecision.closes witness =>
    P.Baseline (capability.reductions.delete input.seed.piece).value ∧
      ¬Target (capability.reductions.delete input.seed.piece).value
  | CT2.DeletionDecision.critical state =>
    ¬(P.Baseline (capability.reductions.delete input.seed.piece).value ∧
        ¬Target (capability.reductions.delete input.seed.piece).value)

            (motive :

                    CT2.sourceObservation capability ctx input context ≠
                      CT2.replacementObservation capability ctx input candidate context) →
                  Sort u_8) →
              (x :
                  Core.FiniteSearch.Result fun context =>
                    CT2.sourceObservation capability ctx input context ≠
                      CT2.replacementObservation capability ctx input candidate context) →
                ((context : capability.contexts.Context (capability.interfaces.interface input.seed.piece)) →
                    (differs :
                        CT2.sourceObservation capability ctx input context ≠
                          CT2.replacementObservation capability ctx input candidate context) →
                      motive (Core.FiniteSearch.Result.found context differs)) →
                  ((noDifference :
                        ∀ (value : capability.contexts.Context (capability.interfaces.interface input.seed.piece)),
                          ¬CT2.sourceObservation capability ctx input value ≠
                              CT2.replacementObservation capability ctx input candidate value) →

internalDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} →

      {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext P Target} →
        {input : CT2.Input capability ctx} →
          {motive : (a a_1 : CT2.Graph.NodeId) → CT2.Graph.Path capability ctx input a a_1 → Sort u} →
            ((node : CT2.Graph.NodeId) → motive node node (CT2.Graph.Path.nil node)) →
              ({first second last : CT2.Graph.NodeId} →
                  (a : CT2.Graph.Edge capability ctx input first second) →
                    (a_1 : CT2.Graph.Path capability ctx input second last) →
                      motive second last a_1 → motive first last (CT2.Graph.Path.cons a a_1)) →
                {a a_1 : CT2.Graph.NodeId} → (t : CT2.Graph.Path capability ctx input a a_1) → motive a a_1 t

  ∃ result,
    CT2.RawOutcome.Valid capability ctx input result.outcome ∧
      CT2.Graph.ValidTrace capability ctx input (CT2.ExecutionResult.trace capability ctx input result)

  left = CT2.run capability ctx input → right = CT2.run capability ctx input → left = right

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {Target : P.Ambient → Prop} (capability : CT2.Capability P Target)

  (result : CT2.ExecutionResult capability ctx input),
  result.terminal = CT2.Graph.Terminal.deletionC2 ∨
    result.terminal = CT2.Graph.Terminal.replacementC2 ∨
      result.terminal = CT2.Graph.Terminal.separating ∨ result.terminal = CT2.Graph.Terminal.criticality

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {α : Type u} (enumeration : FinEnum α) (p : α → Prop) (decideP : (value : α) → Decidable (p value)),
  match Core.FiniteSearch.first enumeration p decideP with
  | Core.FiniteSearch.FirstResult.found hit => hit.value ∈ enumeration.orderedValues ∧ p hit.value
  | Core.FiniteSearch.FirstResult.absent none => ∀ (value : α), ¬p value

internalDeclarations.docString:
Ordered search is sound and exhaustive over the entire enumerated
universe.

              (source : S.Piece) → (candidate : S.Candidate) → Decidable (S.Admissible G source candidate)) →
            ((G : P.Ambient) →
                (source : S.Piece) → (candidate : S.Candidate) → Decidable (S.Smaller G source candidate)) →
              (inputSize : P.Ambient → ℕ) →
                (workCoefficient workDegree : ℕ) →
                  (∀ (G : P.Ambient),
                      CT3.localCheckBound contexts candidates rows ≤ workCoefficient * (inputSize G + 1) ^ workDegree) →
                    CT3.Capability S

            S.response (S.rowPiece row) context ≠ S.rowResponse row context) →

          ((noDefect :
                ∀ (index : S.Row) (value : S.Context),
                  ¬S.response (S.rowPiece index) value ≠ S.rowResponse index value) →
              motive (Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.absent noDefect)) →
            motive x

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT3.Spec P) (input : CT3.Input S) (row : S.Row) (context : S.Context),
  S.response input.piece context ≠ S.rowResponse row context → CT3.RowMatches S input row → False

internalDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  (S : CT3.Spec P) →
    (input : CT3.Input S) →
      (row : S.Row) →
        (motive : Core.ResponseComparison (S.response input.piece) (S.rowResponse row) → Sort u_7) →
          (x : Core.ResponseComparison (S.response input.piece) (S.rowResponse row)) →
            ((allEqual : ∀ (context : S.Context), S.response input.piece context = S.rowResponse row context) →
                motive (Core.ResponseComparison.equal allEqual)) →
              ((context : S.Context) →
                  (differs : S.response input.piece context ≠ S.rowResponse row context) →
                    motive (Core.ResponseComparison.different context differs)) →

internalDeclarations.type:

  (x : Decidable (S.response (S.rowPiece row) context = S.rowResponse row context)),
  (∀ (same : S.response (S.rowPiece row) context = S.rowResponse row context), motive (isTrue same)) →
    (∀ (differs : ¬S.response (S.rowPiece row) context = S.rowResponse row context), motive (isFalse differs)) →
      motive x

internalDeclarations.type:

presentation.plainExplanation: The application-specific size measure used in CT3's complexity certificate.
presentation.label: Input size

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {S : CT3.Spec P} → CT3.Capability S → P.Ambient → ℕ
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Capability.inputSize

presentation.plainExplanation: The coefficient in the certified polynomial bound on CT3 checks.
presentation.label: Work coefficient

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {S : CT3.Spec P} → CT3.Capability S → ℕ
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Capability.workCoefficient

presentation.plainExplanation: The degree in the certified polynomial bound on CT3 checks.
presentation.label: Work degree

formalDeclaration.type: {P : Core.Problem} → {S : CT3.Spec P} → CT3.Capability S → ℕ
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Capability.workDegree

presentation.plainExplanation: A proof that the full context, candidate, and row schedule satisfies the declared bound.
presentation.label: Polynomial work bound

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {S : CT3.Spec P} (self : CT3.Capability S) (G : P.Ambient),
  CT3.localCheckBound self.contexts self.candidates self.rows ≤
    self.workCoefficient * (self.inputSize G + 1) ^ self.workDegree
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.Capability.workBound

presentation.plainExplanation: The union of the mapped atom-side and outside-context edge sets.
presentation.label: Literal graph gluing

formalDeclaration.type:
{T : Type u} →
  FinEnum T → Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Piece T → Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T → Graph.PackedFiniteObject
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue

presentation.plainExplanation: The normalized outside context owns no boundary--boundary edge; the atom side owns those edges.
presentation.label: Boundary-edge ownership

formalDeclaration.type:
∀ {T : Type u} (self : Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context T) (left right : T),
  ¬self.graph.Adj (Sum.inl left) (Sum.inl right)
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.Context.noBoundaryEdge

presentation.plainExplanation: A graph isomorphism identifying the literal gluing with the selected ambient graph.
presentation.label: Graph reconstruction

formalDeclaration.type:
{input : Graph.PackedMinimumDegreeCycle.StaticInput} →
  {T : Type u} →
    {boundaries : FinEnum T} →
      {ctx : Core.MinimalCounterexampleContext input.problem input.Target} →
        (self : Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ProperAtom input boundaries ctx) →
          (Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.glue boundaries self.source self.outside).object.graph ≃g ctx.G.object.graph
formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ProperAtom.reconstruct

presentation.plainExplanation: The literal atom-side graph is connected.
presentation.label: Connected atom

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ProperAtom.connected

presentation.plainExplanation: The reconstructed atom is a proper local support of the ambient graph.
presentation.label: Proper atom

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.ProperAtom.proper

presentation.plainExplanation: The replacement and source lie in the same boundary-degree fibre.
presentation.label: Boundary-degree equality

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.boundaryDegree_eq

presentation.plainExplanation: Every replacement target response in an outside context transports to the source; two-way equivalence is not assumed.
presentation.label: Obstruction inclusion

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.obstructionIncluded

presentation.plainExplanation: The replacement piece alone contains no selected target cycle.
presentation.label: Internal target avoidance

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.internalTargetFree

presentation.plainExplanation: Every internal replacement vertex meets the minimum-degree threshold.
presentation.label: Replacement internal degree

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.internalBaseline

presentation.plainExplanation: Only the replacement piece is assumed smaller; whole-graph decrease is derived.
presentation.label: Local lexicographic decrease

formalDeclaration.name: StructuralExhaustion.Graph.PackedBoundariedGluing.MinimumDegreeCycleReplacement.Compression.locallySmaller

apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.run

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT3.Spec P) (C : CT3.Capability S) (input : CT3.Input S),
  CT3.OutcomeClaim (CT3.run S C input).outcome
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.run_verified

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT3.Spec P) (C : CT3.Capability S) (input : CT3.Input S),
  CT3.Graph.ValidTrace (CT3.run S C input).trace
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.run_trace_valid

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT3.Spec P) (C : CT3.Capability S) (input : CT3.Input S),
  ∃ result, CT3.OutcomeClaim result.outcome ∧ CT3.Graph.ValidTrace result.trace
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.run_total

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT3.Spec P) (C : CT3.Capability S) (input : CT3.Input S)
  (first second : CT3.ExecutionResult S C input),
  first = CT3.run S C input → second = CT3.run S C input → first = second
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.run_deterministic

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} {S : CT3.Spec P} {C : CT3.Capability S} {input : CT3.Input S}
  (result : CT3.ExecutionResult S C input),
  result.terminal = CT3.Graph.Terminal.compression ∨
    result.terminal = CT3.Graph.Terminal.distinguishing ∨
      result.terminal = CT3.Graph.Terminal.knownRow ∨ result.terminal = CT3.Graph.Terminal.novelRow
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT3.outcome_exhaustive

      {input : CT4.Input P} →
        {total : CT4.TotalAssignmentState S capability input} →
          {motive : CT4.FibreDecision S capability input total → Sort u} →
            ((residual : CT4.OverloadedFibreResidual S capability input) →
                motive (CT4.FibreDecision.overloaded residual)) →
              ((state : CT4.BoundedFibreState S capability input) → motive (CT4.FibreDecision.bounded state)) →
                (t : CT4.FibreDecision S capability input total) → motive t
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT4.FibreDecision.rec

  CT4.Graph.ValidTrace S capability input (CT4.ExecutionResult.trace S capability input (CT4.run S capability input))
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT4.run_trace_valid

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT4.Spec P) (capability : CT4.Capability S) (input : CT4.Input P),
  ∃ result,
    CT4.RawOutcome.Valid S capability input result.outcome ∧
      CT4.Graph.ValidTrace S capability input (CT4.ExecutionResult.trace S capability input result)
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT4.run_total

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT4.Spec P) (capability : CT4.Capability S) (input : CT4.Input P)
  (left right : CT4.ExecutionResult S capability input),
  left = CT4.run S capability input → right = CT4.run S capability input → left = right
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT4.run_deterministic

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT4.Spec P) (capability : CT4.Capability S) (input : CT4.Input P)
  (result : CT4.ExecutionResult S capability input),
  result.terminal = CT4.Graph.Terminal.missing ∨
    result.terminal = CT4.Graph.Terminal.overload ∨
      result.terminal = CT4.Graph.Terminal.c4 ∨ result.terminal = CT4.Graph.Terminal.capacity
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT4.outcome_exhaustive

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.firstOnList._proof_1

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {α : Type u_1} (xs : List α) (p : α → Prop) (decideP : (value : α) → Decidable (p value)) (index : ℕ),
  List.findIdx? (fun value => decide (p value)) xs = some index →
    List.findIdx (fun value => decide (p value)) xs = index

internalDeclarations.type:

  List.findIdx? (fun value => decide (p value)) xs = some index →
    ∀ (bound : index < xs.length), decide (p xs[index]) = true

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {α : Type u_1} (xs : List α) (index : ℕ) (bound : index < xs.length),
  xs = List.take index xs ++ xs[index] :: List.drop (index + 1) xs

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {α : Type u_1} (xs : List α) (p : α → Prop) (decideP : (value : α) → Decidable (p value)) (index : ℕ)

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {α : Type u_1} (xs : List α) (p : α → Prop) (decideP : (value : α) → Decidable (p value)) (index : ℕ),
  List.findIdx (fun value => decide (p value)) xs = index → ∀ candidate ∈ List.take index xs, p candidate → False
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.firstOnList._proof_6

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {α : Type u_1} (xs : List α) (p : α → Prop) (decideP : (value : α) → Decidable (p value)),

internalDeclarations.type:
{α : Type u} →
  {p : α → Prop} →
    {motive : Core.FiniteSearch.Result p → Sort u_1} →
      (t : Core.FiniteSearch.Result p) →
        ((value : α) → (holds : p value) → motive (Core.FiniteSearch.Result.found value holds)) →
          ((none : ∀ (value : α), ¬p value) → motive (Core.FiniteSearch.Result.absent none)) → motive t
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.Result.casesOn

internalDeclarations.type: {α : Type u} → {p : α → Prop} → (value : α) → p value → Core.FiniteSearch.Result p

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.Result.none_complete

internalDeclarations.type: {α : Type u} → {p : α → Prop} → Core.FiniteSearch.Result p → Option α
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.Result.value?

internalDeclarations.type: {α : Type u} → FinEnum α → (p : α → Prop) → ((value : α) → Decidable (p value)) → Core.FiniteSearch.Result p

internalDeclarations.type:

    {capability : CT7.Capability S} →
      {ctx : Core.BranchContext P} →
        {input : CT7.Input S ctx} →
          (context : S.Context) →
            context ∈ (capability.contexts ctx input.left input.right).orderedValues →

internalDeclarations.type:

  {input : CT7.Input S ctx},
  (∀ (context : S.Context), context ∈ (capability.contexts ctx input.left input.right).orderedValues) →
    (∀ (context : S.Context), ¬S.Realizes ctx input.left context) → CT7.UnrealizedState S capability ctx input
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT7.UnrealizedState.mk

internalDeclarations.type:

internalDeclarations.type:

                {a a_1 : CT7.Graph.NodeId} → (t : CT7.Graph.Path S capability ctx input a a_1) → motive a a_1 t

          {motive : (a a_1 : CT7.Graph.NodeId) → CT7.Graph.Path S capability ctx input a a_1 → Sort u} →
            {a a_1 : CT7.Graph.NodeId} →
              (t : CT7.Graph.Path S capability ctx input a a_1) →
                ((node : CT7.Graph.NodeId) → motive node node (CT7.Graph.Path.nil node)) →
                  ({a b c : CT7.Graph.NodeId} →
                      (a_2 : CT7.Graph.Edge S capability ctx input a b) →

  (x : Decidable (S.response ctx input.left context = S.response ctx input.right context)),
  (∀ (same : S.response ctx input.left context = S.response ctx input.right context), motive (isTrue same)) →

    (∀

          ¬capability.response (CT8.OrderedRepeatedPair.first capability pair) context =
              capability.response (CT8.OrderedRepeatedPair.second capability pair) context),
        motive (isFalse differs)) →

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.OrderedCollection
internalDeclarations.docString:
A finite ordered collection, without the claim that it contains every
inhabitant of its element type.

This is intentionally a duplicate-free list rather than a `Finset`: first-hit
machine semantics consume its order.  All set-like reasoning should pass
through Mathlib's `List`/`Finset` API instead of adding another collection
implementation here.

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.OrderedCollection
internalDeclarations.docString:
A finite ordered collection, without the claim that it contains every
inhabitant of its element type.

This is intentionally a duplicate-free list rather than a `Finset`: first-hit
machine semantics consume its order.  All set-like reasoning should pass
through Mathlib's `List`/`Finset` API instead of adding another collection
implementation here.

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.OrderedCollection
internalDeclarations.docString:
A finite ordered collection, without the claim that it contains every
inhabitant of its element type.

This is intentionally a duplicate-free list rather than a `Finset`: first-hit
machine semantics consume its order.  All set-like reasoning should pass
through Mathlib's `List`/`Finset` API instead of adding another collection
implementation here.

internalDeclarations.type:

            Sort u_4) →
        (x : Core.FiniteSearch.ListResult input.cells.values fun cell => ¬capability.Admissible input.context cell) →
          ((cell : capability.Cell) →
              (member : cell ∈ input.cells.values) →
                (inadmissible : ¬capability.Admissible input.context cell) →
                  motive (Core.FiniteSearch.ListResult.found cell member inadmissible)) →
            ((noGap : ∀ value ∈ input.cells.values, ¬¬capability.Admissible input.context value) →
                motive (Core.FiniteSearch.ListResult.absent noGap)) →
              motive x

internalDeclarations.type:

internalDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {capability : CT12.Capability P} →
    {motive : (a a_1 : CT12.Graph.NodeId) → CT12.Graph.Edge capability a a_1 → Sort u} →
      {a a_1 : CT12.Graph.NodeId} →
        (t : CT12.Graph.Edge capability a a_1) →
          motive CT12.Graph.NodeId.entry CT12.Graph.NodeId.saturation CT12.Graph.Edge.begin →
            ((state : capability.State 0) →
                motive CT12.Graph.NodeId.saturation CT12.Graph.NodeId.exhaustedTerminal
                  (CT12.Graph.Edge.exhausted state)) →
              ({n : ℕ} →
                  (state : capability.State (n + 1)) →
                    motive CT12.Graph.NodeId.saturation CT12.Graph.NodeId.peel (CT12.Graph.Edge.positive state)) →
                ({n : ℕ} →
                    {state : capability.State (n + 1)} →
                      (peeled : capability.Peeled state) →
                        motive CT12.Graph.NodeId.peel CT12.Graph.NodeId.restoration (CT12.Graph.Edge.peeled peeled)) →
                  ({n : ℕ} →
                      {state : capability.State (n + 1)} →
                        (peeled : capability.Peeled state) →
                          (residual : capability.DemandResidual) →
                            motive CT12.Graph.NodeId.restoration CT12.Graph.NodeId.demandTerminal
                              (CT12.Graph.Edge.demand peeled residual)) →
                    ({n : ℕ} →
                        {state : capability.State (n + 1)} →
                          (peeled : capability.Peeled state) →
                            (residual : capability.TierResidual) →
                              motive CT12.Graph.NodeId.restoration CT12.Graph.NodeId.tierTerminal
                                (CT12.Graph.Edge.tier peeled residual)) →
                      ({n next : ℕ} →
                          {state : capability.State (n + 1)} →
                            (peeled : capability.Peeled state) →
                              (nextState : capability.State next) →
                                (decreases : next < n + 1) →
                                  motive CT12.Graph.NodeId.restoration CT12.Graph.NodeId.decrease
                                    (CT12.Graph.Edge.continue peeled nextState decreases)) →
                        ({n next : ℕ} →
                            {state : capability.State (n + 1)} →
                              (peeled : capability.Peeled state) →
                                (nextState : capability.State next) →
                                  (decreases : next < n + 1) →
                                    motive CT12.Graph.NodeId.decrease CT12.Graph.NodeId.saturation
                                      (CT12.Graph.Edge.loopBack peeled nextState decreases)) →
                          motive a a_1 t

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) (n : ℕ) (state : capability.State (n + 1))
  (x :
    (y : (load : ℕ) ×' capability.State load) →
      InvImage (fun x1 x2 => x1 < x2) (fun x => PSigma.casesOn x fun load state => load) y ⟨n.succ, state⟩ →
        CT12.LoopResult capability y.1 y.2)
  (next : ℕ) (nextState : capability.State next) (decreases : next < n + 1),
  4 * next ≤ 4 * n → 4 + (CT12.Graph.Path.trace capability (x ⟨next, nextState⟩ ⋯).path).length ≤ 4 * (n + 1) + 2
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop._unary._proof_10

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) (n : ℕ) (state : capability.State (n + 1))
  (x :
    (y : (load : ℕ) ×' capability.State load) →
      InvImage (fun x1 x2 => x1 < x2) (fun x => PSigma.casesOn x fun load state => load) y ⟨n.succ, state⟩ →
        CT12.LoopResult capability y.1 y.2)
  (next : ℕ) (nextState : capability.State next) (decreases : next < n + 1),
  4 + (CT12.Graph.Path.trace capability (x ⟨next, nextState⟩ ⋯).path).length ≤ 4 * (n + 1) + 2 →
    (CT12.Graph.Path.trace capability
          (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.positive state)
            (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.peeled (capability.peel state))
              (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.continue (capability.peel state) nextState decreases)
                (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.loopBack (capability.peel state) nextState decreases)
                  (x ⟨next, nextState⟩ ⋯).path))))).length ≤
      4 * (n + 1) + 2
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop._unary._proof_11

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) (state : capability.State 0),
  (CT12.Graph.Path.trace capability
        (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.exhausted state)
          (CT12.Graph.Path.nil CT12.Graph.NodeId.exhaustedTerminal))).length ≤
    4 * 0 + 2
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop._unary._proof_2

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) (n : ℕ) (state : capability.State (n + 1))
  (residual : capability.DemandResidual),
  (CT12.Graph.Path.trace capability
        (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.positive state)
          (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.peeled (capability.peel state))
            (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.demand (capability.peel state) residual)
              (CT12.Graph.Path.nil CT12.Graph.NodeId.demandTerminal))))).length ≤
    4 * (n + 1) + 2
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop._unary._proof_4

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) (n : ℕ) (state : capability.State (n + 1))
  (residual : capability.TierResidual),
  (CT12.Graph.Path.trace capability
        (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.positive state)
          (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.peeled (capability.peel state))
            (CT12.Graph.Path.cons (CT12.Graph.Edge.tier (capability.peel state) residual)
              (CT12.Graph.Path.nil CT12.Graph.NodeId.tierTerminal))))).length ≤
    4 * (n + 1) + 2
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop._unary._proof_5

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) (n : ℕ) (state : capability.State (n + 1)) (next : ℕ)
  (nextState : capability.State next),
  next < n + 1 →
    InvImage (fun x1 x2 => x1 < x2) (fun x => PSigma.casesOn x fun load state => load) ⟨next, nextState⟩ ⟨n.succ, state⟩
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop._unary._proof_6

internalDeclarations.type: ∀ (n next : ℕ), next < n + 1 → next ≤ n
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop._unary._proof_7

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) (n : ℕ) (state : capability.State (n + 1))
  (x :
    (y : (load : ℕ) ×' capability.State load) →
      InvImage (fun x1 x2 => x1 < x2) (fun x => PSigma.casesOn x fun load state => load) y ⟨n.succ, state⟩ →
        CT12.LoopResult capability y.1 y.2)
  (next : ℕ) (nextState : capability.State next) (decreases : next < n + 1),
  next ≤ n → (x ⟨next, nextState⟩ ⋯).iterations.succ ≤ n.succ
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop._unary._proof_8

internalDeclarations.type: ∀ (n next : ℕ), next ≤ n → 4 * next ≤ 4 * n
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop._unary._proof_9

internalDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  (capability : CT12.Capability P) →
    (n : ℕ) →
      (motive :
          CT12.Restoration capability.State capability.DemandResidual capability.TierResidual (n + 1) → Sort u_7) →
        (x : CT12.Restoration capability.State capability.DemandResidual capability.TierResidual (n + 1)) →
          ((residual : capability.DemandResidual) → motive (CT12.Restoration.demand residual)) →
            ((residual : capability.TierResidual) → motive (CT12.Restoration.tier residual)) →
              ((next : ℕ) →
                  (nextState : capability.State next) →
                    (decreases : next < n + 1) → motive (CT12.Restoration.continue next nextState decreases)) →
                motive x
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop.match_1

internalDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  (capability : CT12.Capability P) →
    (motive : (x : ℕ) → capability.State x → Sort u_7) →
      (x : ℕ) →
        (x_1 : capability.State x) →
          ((state : capability.State 0) → motive 0 state) →
            ((n : ℕ) → (state : capability.State (n + 1)) → motive n.succ state) → motive x x_1
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.runLoop.match_3

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.ExhaustedCertificate

internalDeclarations.type:
{State : ℕ → Type uState} →
  {Demand : Type uDemand} →

      {load : ℕ} →
        {motive : CT12.Restoration State Demand Tier load → Sort u} →
          (t : CT12.Restoration State Demand Tier load) →
            ((next : ℕ) →
                (state : State next) →
                  (decreases : next < load) → motive (CT12.Restoration.continue next state decreases)) →
              ((residual : Demand) → motive (CT12.Restoration.demand residual)) →
                ((residual : Tier) → motive (CT12.Restoration.tier residual)) → motive t

internalDeclarations.type:
{State : ℕ → Type uState} →
  {Demand : Type uDemand} →

        {motive : CT12.Restoration State Demand Tier load → Sort u} →
          ((next : ℕ) →
              (state : State next) →
                (decreases : next < load) → motive (CT12.Restoration.continue next state decreases)) →
            ((residual : Demand) → motive (CT12.Restoration.demand residual)) →
              ((residual : Tier) → motive (CT12.Restoration.tier residual)) →
                (t : CT12.Restoration State Demand Tier load) → motive t

  CT12.Outcome.Valid capability (CT12.run capability input).outcome
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.run_verified

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) (input : CT12.Input capability),
  CT12.Graph.ValidTrace capability (CT12.ExecutionResult.trace capability (CT12.run capability input))
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.run_trace_valid

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) (input : CT12.Input capability),
  ∃ r,
    CT12.Outcome.Valid capability r.outcome ∧
      CT12.Graph.ValidTrace capability (CT12.ExecutionResult.trace capability r) ∧
        r.iterations ≤ input.load ∧ (CT12.ExecutionResult.trace capability r).length ≤ 4 * input.load + 3
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.run_total

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) {input : CT12.Input capability}
  (l r : CT12.ExecutionResult capability input), l = CT12.run capability input → r = CT12.run capability input → l = r
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.run_deterministic

apiDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (capability : CT12.Capability P) {input : CT12.Input capability}
  (result : CT12.ExecutionResult capability input),
  result.terminal = CT12.Graph.Terminal.exhausted ∨
    result.terminal = CT12.Graph.Terminal.demand ∨ result.terminal = CT12.Graph.Terminal.tier
apiDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT12.outcome_exhaustive

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {α : Type u} (cost : α → ℕ) (x : α) (x_1 : List α),
  CT13.selectMin cost x x_1 =
    match x, x_1 with
    | current, [] => current
    | current, head :: tail => CT13.selectMin cost (if cost head < cost current then head else current) tail
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT13.selectMin.eq_def

internalDeclarations.type: ∀ {α : Type u} (cost : α → ℕ) (current : α) (values : List α), cost (CT13.selectMin cost current values) ≤ cost current
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT13.selectMin_le_current

internalDeclarations.type: ∀ {α : Type u_1} {b : α} {l : List α} {a : α}, (a ∈ b :: l) = (a = b ∨ a ∈ l)
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT13.selectMin_le_of_mem._simp_1_2

internalDeclarations.type:
{P : Core.Problem} →
  {C : CT13.Capability P} →
    {ctx : Core.BranchContext P} →
      CT13.TierOneAbsenceState C ctx →
        (selected : C.Obstruction) →
          selected = CT13.selectMin (C.obstructionCost ctx) C.fallbackDefault C.obstructions.orderedValues →
            (∀ (obstruction : C.Obstruction), C.obstructionCost ctx selected ≤ C.obstructionCost ctx obstruction) →
              CT13.FallbackState C ctx
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT13.FallbackState.mk

internalDeclarations.type: Type u → Type u
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.OrderedCollection
internalDeclarations.docString:
A finite ordered collection, without the claim that it contains every
inhabitant of its element type.

This is intentionally a duplicate-free list rather than a `Finset`: first-hit
machine semantics consume its order.  All set-like reasoning should pass
through Mathlib's `List`/`Finset` API instead of adding another collection
implementation here.

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.firstOnList._proof_1

internalDeclarations.type:

    List.findIdx (fun value => decide (p value)) xs = index
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.firstOnList._proof_2

internalDeclarations.type:

  List.findIdx? (fun value => decide (p value)) xs = some index →
    ∀ (bound : index < xs.length), decide (p xs[index]) = true

internalDeclarations.type:

internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.firstOnList._proof_4

steps.plainExplanation:
A decision procedure for strict inequality of natural numbers, usually accessed via the
`DecidableLT Nat` instance.

Examples:
 * `(if 3 < 4 then "yes" else "no") = "yes"`
 * `(if 4 < 4 then "yes" else "no") = "no"`
 * `(if 6 < 4 then "yes" else "no") = "no"`
 * `show 5 < 12 by decide`

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT15.Spec P) (capability : CT15.Capability S) (input : CT15.Input P)
  (rank : CT15.RankState S capability input),
  match CT15.splitRank S capability input rank with
  | CT15.RankSplitDecision.dropped residual =>
    S.TargetDependent input residual.hit.value ∧ ∀ coordinate ∈ residual.hit.before, ¬S.TargetDependent input coordinate
  | CT15.RankSplitDecision.full state =>
    (∀ (coordinate : S.Coordinate), ¬S.TargetDependent input coordinate) ∧ rank.value = CT15.targetRank S capability
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT15.splitRank_sound

              ({a b z : CT16.Graph.NodeId} →
                  (a_2 : CT16.Graph.Edge C ctx a b) →
                    (a_3 : CT16.Graph.Path C ctx b z) → motive a z (CT16.Graph.Path.cons a_2 a_3)) →
                motive a a_1 t

  (input : CT17.Input S capability ctx),
  match CT17.analyzeCompatibility S capability ctx input with
  | CT17.CompatibilityDecision.incompatible residual => ¬S.Compatible ctx residual.target residual.offset
  | CT17.CompatibilityDecision.compatible _state =>
    ∀ (target : S.Target) (offset : S.Offset), S.Compatible ctx target offset

  (input : CT17.Input S capability ctx) (compatible : CT17.CompatibleState S capability ctx input),
  match CT17.analyzeScale S capability ctx input compatible with
  | CT17.ScaleDecision.finite _state => input.scale ≤ capability.finiteScaleLimit
  | CT17.ScaleDecision.orbit _state => capability.finiteScaleLimit < input.scale

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT17.Spec P) (capability : CT17.Capability S) (ctx : Core.BranchContext P)
  (input : CT17.Input S capability ctx) {compatible : CT17.CompatibleState S capability ctx input}
  (finite : CT17.FiniteScaleState S capability ctx input compatible),
  match CT17.analyzeSurvivors S capability ctx input finite with
  | CT17.SurvivorDecision.exhausted _certificate =>
    ∀ (position : S.Position input.scale), ¬CT17.Survives S capability ctx input position
  | CT17.SurvivorDecision.survivors residual =>
    residual.enumeration.survivors = residual.first :: residual.remaining ∧
      ∀ (position : S.Position input.scale),
        position ∈ residual.enumeration.survivors ↔ CT17.Survives S capability ctx input position
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT17.analyzeSurvivors_sound

internalDeclarations.type:
∀ {P : Core.Problem} (S : CT17.Spec P) (capability : CT17.Capability S) (ctx : Core.BranchContext P)
  (input : CT17.Input S capability ctx) {compatible : CT17.CompatibleState S capability ctx input}
  (orbit : CT17.OrbitScaleState S capability ctx input compatible),
  match CT17.analyzeArithmetic S capability ctx input orbit with
  | CT17.ArithmeticDecision.targetHit certificate =>
    S.orbitValue ctx input.scale certificate.offset = S.targetValue certificate.target
  | CT17.ArithmeticDecision.residual residual =>
    residual.values = List.map (S.orbitValue ctx input.scale) capability.offsets.orderedValues ∧
      CT17.OrbitAvoids S capability ctx input
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.CT17.analyzeArithmetic_sound

  (∀ index ∈ enumeration.indices.orderedValues, ∀ (value : α index), ¬p index value) →
    ∀ (index : ι) (value : α index), ¬p index value
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.dependentSearch._proof_1

internalDeclarations.type:
{ι : Type u_1} →
  {α : ι → Type u_2} →
    (enumeration : Core.DependentEnumeration ι α) →
      (p : (i : ι) → α i → Prop) →
        (motive :
            StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentListResult✝ enumeration.indices.orderedValues p →
              Sort u_3) →
          (x : StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentListResult✝ enumeration.indices.orderedValues p) →
            ((index : ι) →
                (member : index ∈ enumeration.indices.orderedValues) →
                  (value : α index) →
                    (holds : p index value) →
                      motive
                        (StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentListResult.found✝ index member value holds)) →
              ((absentProof : ∀ index ∈ enumeration.indices.orderedValues, ∀ (value : α index), ¬p index value) →
                  motive (StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.DependentListResult.absent✝ absentProof)) →
                motive x
internalDeclarations.name: StructuralExhaustion.Core.FiniteSearch.dependentSearch.match_1

          (position : S.Position input.scale) →
            (motive :
                (Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult fun target offset =>
                    S.blockValue ctx position offset = S.targetValue target) →
                  Sort u_7) →
              (x :
                  Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult fun target offset =>
                    S.blockValue ctx position offset = S.targetValue target) →
                ((target : S.Target) →
                    (offset : S.Offset) →
                      (equal : S.blockValue ctx position offset = S.targetValue target) →
                        motive (Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.found target offset equal)) →
                  ((absentProof :
                        ∀ (index : S.Target) (value : S.Offset),
                          ¬S.blockValue ctx position value = S.targetValue index) →
                      motive (Core.FiniteSearch.DependentResult.absent absentProof)) →
                    motive x
